key_judgments.judgment: Israel territorial expansion in Gaza during nominal ceasefire period establishes facts-on-the-ground for future annexation claims and constrains any future Palestinian state to non-contiguous enclaves. This pattern mirrors West Bank settlement expansion dynamics and signals long-term territorial consolidation intent.

Hamas rejection of Board of Peace disarmament plan and Israeli cabinet deliberations on resuming full-scale operations signal structural collapse of diplomatic track. Impasse is structural, not episodic, given three-plus reporting cycles of failed disarmament talks. US diplomatic de-weighting applies: Board of Peace proposals carry lower I5 weight than pre-2026 US-brokered frameworks.

Iran strikes on UAE Fujairah petroleum zone on 4-5 May break 8 April ceasefire restraint and signal willingness to resume offensive operations against Gulf states despite nominal ceasefire framework. Post-ceasefire strikes on UAE represent discrete escalatory event; requires monitoring across subsequent reporting cycles to determine if this signals structural resumption of Gulf targeting.

Sudan multi-front expansion (Blue Nile, Kordofan) with manned airstrikes and SAF gold mine strikes targeting RSF revenue represent structural operational tempo increase in year-4 war. Early-warning of partition risk over episodic flare. Gulf GDP contraction projections directly constrain external funding for both SAF and RSF, accelerating resource competition and increasing likelihood of territorial partition along resource-rich zones.

Middle East regional pressure and shifting supply networks are materially reshaping Sudan conflict dynamics, with Iran-adjacent actors adjusting arms and resource flows. Sudan-Iran linkage is structurally underweighted in mainstream conflict analysis, which treats Sudan as an isolated civil war rather than a node in broader Middle East proxy competition.

Houthi warnings of Red Sea intervention if Iran attacked are operationally credible given prior anti-ship missile attacks on US Navy vessels. Iran pressing Houthis toward Red Sea attacks if US escalates signals proxy activation as deliberate escalation tool. Threatens 10% global seaborne trade via Bab al-Mandeb. Mainstream coverage treats Houthi warnings as rhetorical posturing rather than operational signaling, underweighting the structural linkage between Iran escalation and Red Sea disruption.

US diplomatic de-weighting in multilateral frameworks creates structural gap in crisis management capacity. European, Gulf, and AU mediation channels lack equivalent convening power or enforcement mechanisms. This gap increases likelihood of ceasefire collapse and escalation resumption. FM-SCEM-04: US withdrawal from multilateral institutions means US participation is no longer a reliable I5 positive in 2026.

weekly_brief:
## Lead Signal

Sudan civil war enters multi-front expansion phase as RSF and SPLM-N advance on Blue Nile capital Damazin while SAF targets RSF gold mines and resumes manned airstrikes in Kordofan. The Sudan civil war has escalated structurally in late April 2026 with the opening of a new Blue Nile front as RSF forces and the SPLM-N faction captured the Al-Keili area on 25 April and advanced northward toward the state capital Damazin, bordering Ethiopia. Simultaneously, SAF-aligned forces launched an offensive across North Kordofan on 18 April to secure the El Obeid-Kadugli road, and the SAF resumed manned aircraft bombing of RSF targets around Dilling on 22 April. In a deliberate economic warfare escalation, the SAF began targeting RSF-controlled gold mines in the South Darfur-South Sudan borderlands in late April, aiming to degrade RSF operational funding. The multi-front expansion, combined with intensifying drone strikes on civilian areas, signals a structural shift in operational tempo rather than an episodic flare, deepening partition risk as both sides contest new terrain. Displacement has exceeded 4 million refugees, with famine conditions in multiple states. This represents the most significant Sudan escalation since the war entered its fourth year, with theatre intensity at I3 worsening and escalation velocity high per the theatre risk matrix.

The Sudan developments dominate this week's signal due to their multi-domain nature--territorial, economic, and humanitarian--coupled with the collapse of Quad mediation, leaving no functioning crisis management channel. RSF and SPLM-N advance threatens Ethiopia spillover, while SAF gold mine strikes risk South Sudan cross-border escalation, elevating the proxy conflict spillover vector to HIGH.

## Other Developments

**Russia resumes mass strikes after Orthodox Easter ceasefire; advances in Sumy, Kharkiv, Donetsk, and Zaporizhia.** Following a short Easter ceasefire on 11-12 April, Russia resumed large-scale strikes on Ukrainian cities, killing at least 58 civilians across multiple oblasts in the week of 11-17 April. Russian forces simultaneously advanced along the international border in Sumy region, captured two settlements near Vovchansk in Kharkiv, made advances near Pokrovsk in Donetsk, and seized a village near Huliaipole in Zaporizhia. ACLED analysis assesses Russia is using intense strike bursts to weaken Kyiv negotiating position and internal coherence ahead of summer diplomatic pressure. This represents a structural I3 worsening post-ceasefire collapse in the Russia-Ukraine theatre, with escalation velocity high and deterrence stability fragile.

**Korean Peninsula nuclear acceleration ordered with 7 tests in 2026 to April as US asset redeployment reduces deterrence posture.** Reasoner analysis confirms the Korean Peninsula remains at elevated I1/I2/I3 levels with nuclear acceleration ordered and 7 tests conducted in 2026 to April. US asset redeployment to the Middle East has reduced deterrence posture in the Indo-Pacific, creating a structural gap in extended deterrence credibility. Nuclear threshold pressure vector stays ELEVATED, with theatre intensity I3 worsening and escalation velocity high; this underweighted development highlights a classic deterrence stability failure pattern.

**Myanmar SCEF resistance alliance formation potentially transformative.** Formation of the Spring Coalition of Ethnic Forces unites multiple ethnic armed organizations and resistance groups under a coordinated framework. While no new weekly data available this cycle, SCEF formation signals a structural shift in resistance capacity that could alter Myanmar civil war stalemate dynamics at I3 stable intensity, with deterrence stability collapsed and medium escalation velocity.

**Proxy spillover risks elevate to HIGH with Sudan fronts and Iran-Houthi coordination.** Sudan Blue Nile expansion threatens Ethiopia spillover via SPLM-N ties; SAF gold mine strikes risk South Sudan escalation. Iran presses Houthis for renewed Red Sea attacks if US escalates, threatening 10 percent of global seaborne trade via Bab al-Mandeb. Proxy conflict spillover vector changed to HIGH this cycle.

## Cross-Monitor Connections

Sudan multi-front expansion and 4 million displacement link to ERM climate-conflict drivers, as mass inflows into Chad and South Sudan generate secondary stress in fragile Sahelian communities. Underreported Sudan information vacuum per ACLED/ICG creates FIMI exploitation conditions, tying to FCW information warfare monitor. SAF gold mine strikes as economic warfare connect to GMM sanctions evasion via RSF UAE gold networks. Korean Peninsula deterrence gap from US Middle East redeployment echoes ESA European security strains on NATO eastern flank cohesion at MODERATE. Myanmar SCEF formation and Yemen AQAP drone/IED surge against STC (highest since November 2022) signal AIM autonomous weapons integration in non-state actor operations. Israel-Lebanon ceasefire unraveling with I6 RED displacement ties to WDM conflict-democracy nexus via governance collapse risks.

## Outlook

Next week, monitor Sudan for Damazin front outcomes and gold mine strike effects on RSF funding; Russia-Ukraine summer diplomacy leverage from post-Easter gains; DPRK test tempo amid US Indo-Pacific posture gaps. Watch proxy activations like Houthi Red Sea threats or Ethiopia spillover. Diplomatic channel attrition at ELEVATED across Sudan Quad collapse, Easter ceasefire failure, Iran impasse persists without breakthroughs. Hybrid warfare intensity ELEVATED with drone/civilian strikes in Sudan/Ukraine/Yemen. Theatre risk matrix shows worsening in Sudan, Russia-Ukraine, Korea, Israel-Lebanon; stability fragile or collapsed in most I3+ theatres.

signal.headline: Israel expands territorial control to 58% of Gaza as Hamas rejects disarmament; April deadliest month of 2026

module_0.title: Israel expands territorial control to 58% of Gaza as Hamas rejects disarmament; April deadliest month of 2026
module_0.body: Israel now controls approximately 58% of Gaza territory, up from 53% under the original ceasefire map, as the Yellow Line has been pushed progressively westward. April 2026 was the deadliest month in Gaza since January, with more than 140 reported fatalities and a 35% increase in violent events. Hamas rejected the Board of Peace disarmament plan in mid-April, submitting a counter-offer tying disarmament to guarantees of Palestinian statehood. Israel's cabinet is actively considering resuming full-scale operations. The diplomatic track is effectively suspended pending Israeli electoral and military calculations.

mainstream.headline: Israel expands Gaza territorial control to 58% as April becomes deadliest month of 2026
mainstream.why_it_matters: The progressive westward expansion of the Yellow Line from 53% to 58% of Gaza territory, combined with April recording more than 140 fatalities and a 35% increase in violent events, signals that the ceasefire framework is collapsing under sustained Israeli military pressure. This territorial consolidation ahead of October 2026 Israeli elections suggests operational tempo is being shaped by domestic political calculus rather than diplomatic restraint. The Board of Peace disarmament mechanism has failed to constrain Israeli expansion, and the cabinet is now actively considering resumption of full-scale operations.
mainstream.body: The progressive westward expansion of the Yellow Line from 53% to 58% of Gaza territory, combined with April recording more than 140 fatalities and a 35% increase in violent events, signals that the ceasefire framework is collapsing under sustained Israeli military pressure. This territorial consolidation ahead of October 2026 Israeli elections suggests operational tempo is being shaped by domestic political calculus rather than diplomatic restraint. The Board of Peace disarmament mechanism has failed to constrain Israeli expansion, and the cabinet is now actively considering resumption of full-scale operations.
mainstream.asymmetric: Territorial expansion during a nominal ceasefire period reveals that Israeli strategy prioritizes facts-on-the-ground over diplomatic process. The 5% territorial gain in under two months establishes a baseline for future annexation claims and constrains any future Palestinian state to non-contiguous enclaves. This pattern mirrors West Bank settlement expansion dynamics and signals long-term territorial consolidation intent.

mainstream.headline: Hamas rejects Board of Peace disarmament plan; diplomatic track suspended
mainstream.why_it_matters: Hamas formally rejected the Board of Peace disarmament plan in mid-April, conditioning any disarmament on guarantees of a sovereign Palestinian state, a halt to Israeli westward expansion, and a complete ceasefire. Israel responded by informing the Board of Peace it would not withdraw from the Yellow Line. The diplomatic track is effectively suspended pending Israeli electoral and military calculations. This impasse removes the primary mechanism for de-escalation and increases the probability of resumed full-scale operations.
mainstream.body: Hamas formally rejected the Board of Peace disarmament plan in mid-April, conditioning any disarmament on guarantees of a sovereign Palestinian state, a halt to Israeli westward expansion, and a complete ceasefire. Israel responded by informing the Board of Peace it would not withdraw from the Yellow Line. The diplomatic track is effectively suspended pending Israeli electoral and military calculations. This impasse removes the primary mechanism for de-escalation and increases the probability of resumed full-scale operations.
mainstream.asymmetric: The Board of Peace is a US-adjacent mechanism operating under structurally discounted diplomatic weight per 2026 calibration rules. Hamas use of disarmament talks to delay escalation ahead of Israeli elections is a documented political warfare tactic. The impasse is structural, not episodic, given three-plus reporting cycles of failed disarmament talks. US diplomatic de-weighting applies: Board of Peace proposals carry lower I5 weight than pre-2026 US-brokered frameworks.

mainstream.headline: Iran strikes UAE Fujairah petroleum zone and Oman on 4-5 May, breaking post-ceasefire restraint
mainstream.why_it_matters: On 4 and 5 May 2026, Iran targeted the United Arab Emirates for the first time since the 8 April ceasefire between Tehran and Washington took effect. The attacks ignited a fire at the Fujairah Petroleum Industries Zone and injured three Indian nationals; a parallel strike hit a residential area in Oman, injuring two expatriates. The targeting of UAE reflects Abu Dhabi alignment with Israel and the United States and signals Iran willingness to resume offensive operations against Gulf states despite the nominal ceasefire framework. Fujairah is a major bunkering and oil storage hub with global shipping implications.
mainstream.body: On 4 and 5 May 2026, Iran targeted the United Arab Emirates for the first time since the 8 April ceasefire between Tehran and Washington took effect. The attacks ignited a fire at the Fujairah Petroleum Industries Zone and injured three Indian nationals; a parallel strike hit a residential area in Oman, injuring two expatriates. The targeting of UAE reflects Abu Dhabi alignment with Israel and the United States and signals Iran willingness to resume offensive operations against Gulf states despite the nominal ceasefire framework. Fujairah is a major bunkering and oil storage hub with global shipping implications.
mainstream.asymmetric: Post-ceasefire strikes on UAE represent a discrete escalatory event rather than a sustained pattern; requires monitoring across subsequent reporting cycles to determine if this signals structural resumption of Gulf targeting. Iranian state media framing of strikes as defensive retaliation constitutes active FIMI operation targeting Gulf and Western audiences. Strike on Fujairah Petroleum Industries Zone directly threatens Gulf energy export infrastructure.

underweighted.headline: Middle East tensions reshape Sudan war economy as Iran-linked supply networks shift; Gulf GDP contraction projections confirmed
underweighted.why_it_matters: ACLED analysis published 6 May 2026 confirms that Middle East regional pressure and shifting supply networks are materially reshaping Sudan conflict dynamics, with Iran-adjacent actors adjusting arms and resource flows. Separately, projections indicate Qatar and Kuwait could face GDP contractions of up to 14% if the Iran conflict continues, while Saudi Arabia and UAE face declines of approximately 3% and 5% respectively. This economic pressure is constraining Gulf states capacity to fund proxy positions in Sudan and other theatres. The Sudan-Iran linkage is structurally underweighted in mainstream conflict analysis, which treats Sudan as an isolated civil war rather than a node in broader Middle East proxy competition.
underweighted.body: ACLED analysis published 6 May 2026 confirms that Middle East regional pressure and shifting supply networks are materially reshaping Sudan conflict dynamics, with Iran-adjacent actors adjusting arms and resource flows. Separately, projections indicate Qatar and Kuwait could face GDP contractions of up to 14% if the Iran conflict continues, while Saudi Arabia and UAE face declines of approximately 3% and 5% respectively. This economic pressure is constraining Gulf states capacity to fund proxy positions in Sudan and other theatres. The Sudan-Iran linkage is structurally underweighted in mainstream conflict analysis, which treats Sudan as an isolated civil war rather than a node in broader Middle East proxy competition.
underweighted.asymmetric: Sudan multi-front expansion (Blue Nile, Kordofan) with manned airstrikes represents structural operational tempo increase in year-4 war; early-warning of partition risk over episodic flare. SAF gold mine strikes introduce deliberate economic warfare targeting RSF revenue; deviation from prior kinetic focus signals funding degradation strategy. Gulf GDP contraction projections directly constrain external funding for both SAF and RSF, accelerating resource competition and increasing likelihood of territorial partition along resource-rich zones.

underweighted.headline: Houthis warn of Red Sea intervention if Iran attacked; Iran presses for renewed shipping attacks
underweighted.why_it_matters: Iran-backed Houthis issued a military intervention warning if regional states ally against Iran, Red Sea used for hostilities, or escalation against Axis of Resistance continues. Iran is reportedly urging Houthis to resume attacks on Red Sea shipping if US escalates against Iran, despite internal Houthi divisions. This proxy activation risks global trade and energy supply shocks. The Red Sea chokepoint carries approximately 10% of global seaborne trade via Bab al-Mandeb. Mainstream coverage treats Houthi warnings as rhetorical posturing rather than operational signaling, underweighting the structural linkage between Iran escalation and Red Sea disruption.
underweighted.body: Iran-backed Houthis issued a military intervention warning if regional states ally against Iran, Red Sea used for hostilities, or escalation against Axis of Resistance continues. Iran is reportedly urging Houthis to resume attacks on Red Sea shipping if US escalates against Iran, despite internal Houthi divisions. This proxy activation risks global trade and energy supply shocks. The Red Sea chokepoint carries approximately 10% of global seaborne trade via Bab al-Mandeb. Mainstream coverage treats Houthi warnings as rhetorical posturing rather than operational signaling, underweighting the structural linkage between Iran escalation and Red Sea disruption.
underweighted.asymmetric: Houthi warnings are operationally credible given prior anti-ship missile attacks on US Navy vessels. Iran pressing Houthis toward Red Sea attacks if US escalates signals proxy activation as a deliberate escalation tool. Potential US move on Kharg Island could destabilize energy markets. Threatens 10% global seaborne trade via Bab al-Mandeb. Ongoing FIMI operations in Lebanon and Red Sea information space amplify both Houthi and Iranian narratives.
underweighted.source_label: Council on Foreign Relations Global Conflict Tracker

vectors.name: Alliance Cohesion Stress

Iran strikes on UAE signal willingness to target Gulf states despite nominal ceasefire framework.

vectors.name: Diplomatic Channel Attrition
vectors.headline: Hamas-Board of Peace disarmament talks reach impasse; diplomatic track suspended
vectors.summary: Hamas formally rejected the Board of Peace disarmament plan in mid-April, conditioning any disarmament on guarantees of a sovereign Palestinian state, a halt to Israeli westward expansion, and a complete ceasefire. Israel responded by informing the Board of Peace it would not withdraw from the Yellow Line. The diplomatic track is effectively suspended pending Israeli electoral and military calculations. This impasse removes the primary mechanism for de-escalation and increases the probability of resumed full-scale operations.
vectors.detail: Hamas formally rejected the Board of Peace disarmament plan in mid-April, conditioning any disarmament on guarantees of a sovereign Palestinian state, a halt to Israeli westward expansion, and a complete ceasefire. Israel responded by informing the Board of Peace it would not withdraw from the Yellow Line. The diplomatic track is effectively suspended pending Israeli electoral and military calculations. This impasse removes the primary mechanism for de-escalation and increases the probability of resumed full-scale operations. US diplomatic de-weighting applies: Board of Peace proposals carry lower I5 weight than pre-2026 US-brokered frameworks. Impasse is structural, not episodic, given three-plus reporting cycles of failed disarmament talks.
vectors.asymmetric: Back-channel activity is rarely verifiable; treat as Unverified unless 2+ independent T2 sources confirm. Ambassador recall (actor-initiated, signaling) vs. expulsion (host-initiated, higher escalation indicator) — code differently. FM-SCEM-04: US withdrawal from multilateral institutions means US participation is no longer a reliable I5 positive in 2026.

vectors.name: Hybrid Warfare Intensity
vectors.headline: Iranian state media framing of UAE strikes as defensive retaliation constitutes active FIMI operation
vectors.summary: Iranian state media framing of strikes on UAE as defensive retaliation constitutes active FIMI operation targeting Gulf and Western audiences. Disarmament impasse is being actively exploited in information space by both Hamas and Israeli government actors; FIMI operations likely amplifying both narratives. Ongoing FIMI operations in Lebanon and Red Sea information space amplify both Houthi and Iranian narratives.

vectors.name: Proxy Conflict Spillover
vectors.headline: Middle East tensions reshape Sudan war economy as Iran-linked supply networks shift
vectors.summary: ACLED analysis published 6 May 2026 confirms that Middle East regional pressure and shifting supply networks are materially reshaping Sudan conflict dynamics, with Iran-adjacent actors adjusting arms and resource flows. Gulf GDP contraction projections directly constrain external funding for both SAF and RSF, accelerating resource competition. Houthis warn of Red Sea intervention if Iran attacked; Iran presses for renewed shipping attacks. Threatens 10% global seaborne trade via Bab al-Mandeb.

Sudan-Iran linkage structurally underweighted in mainstream conflict analysis, which treats Sudan as isolated civil war rather than node in broader Middle East proxy competition.

items.title: Houthis warn of Red Sea intervention if Iran attacked; Iran presses for renewed shipping attacks
items.summary: Iran-backed Houthis issued a military intervention warning if regional states ally against Iran, Red Sea used for hostilities, or escalation against Axis of Resistance continues. Iran is reportedly urging Houthis to resume attacks on Red Sea shipping if US escalates against Iran, despite internal Houthi divisions. This proxy activation risks global trade and energy supply shocks. The Red Sea chokepoint carries approximately 10% of global seaborne trade via Bab al-Mandeb. Houthi warnings are operationally credible given prior anti-ship missile attacks on US Navy vessels.
items.asymmetric: Mainstream coverage treats Houthi warnings as rhetorical posturing rather than operational signaling, underweighting the structural linkage between Iran escalation and Red Sea disruption. Iran pressing Houthis toward Red Sea attacks if US escalates signals proxy activation as deliberate escalation tool. Potential US move on Kharg Island could destabilize energy markets.

items.title: AQAP drone and IED attacks surge against STC forces in Yemen
items.summary: AQAP political violence hit highest since 2022 using drones and IEDs vs STC affiliates in Abyan and Shawba; possible external support. Escalates I2 in Iran-linked Yemen (Red Sea) under Iran theatre. AQAP used drones and IEDs; highest monthly violence since November 2022. Drone use signals reassertion amid Houthi tensions. Links to broader Iran proxy dynamics.
items.asymmetric: AQAP drone use signals reassertion amid Houthi tensions. External support speculated. Links to broader Iran proxy dynamics.

Gulf GDP contraction projections directly constrain external funding for both SAF and RSF, accelerating resource competition. The Sudan-Iran linkage is structurally underweighted in mainstream conflict analysis, which treats Sudan as an isolated civil war rather than a node in broader Middle East proxy competition.

module_9.title: Sanctions, Economic Coercion, and Legal Frameworks

new_developments.title: Gulf GDP contraction projections: Qatar and Kuwait up to 14%, Saudi Arabia 3%, UAE 5%
new_developments.summary: Projections indicate Qatar and Kuwait could face GDP contractions of up to 14% if the Iran conflict continues, while Saudi Arabia and UAE face declines of approximately 3% and 5% respectively. This economic pressure is constraining Gulf states capacity to fund proxy positions in Sudan and other theatres. Strike on Fujairah Petroleum Industries Zone directly threatens Gulf energy export infrastructure; Fujairah is a major bunkering and oil storage hub with global shipping implications.
new_developments.asymmetric: Gulf GDP contraction projections directly constrain external funding for both SAF and RSF in Sudan, accelerating resource competition and increasing likelihood of territorial partition along resource-rich zones. Sustained conflict in Gaza and Israeli blockade maintain upward pressure on regional food and fuel import costs for neighboring economies.

Territorial consolidation ahead of October 2026 Israeli elections suggests operational tempo is being shaped by domestic political calculus rather than diplomatic restraint.

stability_health_composite.note: Stability health composite deteriorating across all components. Diplomatic channel health at lowest level due to Board of Peace failure, Hamas-Israel impasse, and Iran-US ceasefire fragility. Deterrence stability collapsed in Gaza, Sudan, and Israel-Lebanon theatres. Escalation risk aggregate elevated by multi-theatre worsening (Gaza, Iran, Sudan, Ukraine, Lebanon, Korea). Alliance cohesion under stress from US diplomatic de-weighting and Gulf GDP contraction constraining proxy funding.

gaps_register.description: IDMC direct data not retrieved this cycle due to search limit constraints. Crisis Group figures are consistent with prior IDMC baselines, but direct IDMC confirmation would upgrade confidence on Sudan displacement figures from High to Confirmed.

DPRK nuclear test count (7 tests in 2026 to April) is from reasoner analysis without direct weekly research confirmation. Tier 1 or Tier 2 source confirmation (e.g., CTBTO, 38 North, CSIS Missile Tracker) would upgrade confidence from Assessed to High.

US asset redeployment to Middle East is from reasoner analysis without direct weekly research confirmation. Tier 1 or Tier 2 source confirmation (e.g., DoD press release, IISS Military Balance update) would upgrade confidence from Assessed to High.

Iran nuclear talks impasse and April 27 decouple proposal rejection are from reasoner analysis without direct weekly research confirmation. Tier 1 or Tier 2 source confirmation (e.g., IAEA statement, State Department briefing) would upgrade confidence from Assessed to High.

DRC Switzerland talks status is from reasoner analysis without direct weekly research confirmation. Tier 1 or Tier 2 source confirmation (e.g., UN MONUSCO report, ICG update) would upgrade confidence from Assessed to High.

Gaza ceasefire status and Hamas disarmament rejection are from reasoner analysis without direct weekly research confirmation. Tier 1 or Tier 2 source confirmation (e.g., UN OCHA report, Reuters) would upgrade confidence from Assessed to High.

Israel-Lebanon ceasefire unraveling status is from reasoner analysis without direct weekly research confirmation. Tier 1 or Tier 2 source confirmation (e.g., UNIFIL report, Reuters) would upgrade confidence from Assessed to High.

SCEF formation in Myanmar is from reasoner analysis without direct weekly research confirmation. Tier 1 or Tier 2 source confirmation (e.g., ICG Myanmar update, ACLED Myanmar page) would upgrade confidence from Assessed to High.

active_escalation_events.event: Hamas formally rejects Board of Peace disarmament plan; Israel cabinet begins deliberations on resuming full-scale operations
active_escalation_events.strategic_significance: Removes primary mechanism for de-escalation and increases probability of resumed full-scale operations. Impasse is structural, not episodic, given three-plus reporting cycles of failed disarmament talks.

active_escalation_events.event: Iran strikes UAE Fujairah petroleum zone, breaking 8 April ceasefire restraint
active_escalation_events.strategic_significance: First Iranian strikes on UAE territory since 8 April ceasefire; signals willingness to resume offensive operations against Gulf states despite nominal ceasefire framework. Fujairah is major bunkering and oil storage hub with global shipping implications.

active_escalation_events.event: Sudan Armed Forces introduce gold mine strikes targeting RSF revenue in Kordofan
active_escalation_events.strategic_significance: Deviation from prior kinetic focus signals funding degradation strategy. Gulf GDP contraction projections directly constrain external funding for both SAF and RSF, accelerating resource competition and increasing likelihood of territorial partition along resource-rich zones.

f_flag_history.detail: Unverified ceasefire claims; Hamas rejection. Impasse is structural, not episodic, given three-plus reporting cycles of failed disarmament talks.
F4 on I5; talks produced protocols but fighting continues. Switzerland talks interim (AMBER, not RED expulsion).
Impasse confirmed; no verification of decouple proposal progress. Impasse is AMBER-equivalent (talks stalled, not expelled).
Ongoing Hormuz closure and Gulf attacks contradict dismantled claim.
No new data contradicting limited framing inconsistency; Operation Eternal Darkness scale persists.

open_findings.headline: Israel expands Gaza territorial control to 58%; April deadliest month of 2026 with 140+ fatalities
open_findings.why_it_matters: Territorial expansion during nominal ceasefire period establishes facts-on-the-ground for future annexation claims and constrains any future Palestinian state to non-contiguous enclaves. Pattern mirrors West Bank settlement expansion dynamics.

open_findings.headline: Hamas rejects Board of Peace disarmament plan; diplomatic track suspended
open_findings.why_it_matters: Removes primary mechanism for de-escalation and increases probability of resumed full-scale operations. Impasse is structural, not episodic, given three-plus reporting cycles of failed disarmament talks.

open_findings.headline: Iran strikes UAE Fujairah petroleum zone on 4-5 May, breaking 8 April ceasefire restraint
open_findings.why_it_matters: First Iranian strikes on UAE territory since 8 April ceasefire; signals willingness to resume offensive operations against Gulf states despite nominal ceasefire framework. Fujairah is major bunkering and oil storage hub with global shipping implications.

open_findings.headline: Sudan multi-front expansion with SAF gold mine strikes targeting RSF revenue
open_findings.why_it_matters: Multi-front expansion (Blue Nile, Kordofan) with manned airstrikes represents structural operational tempo increase in year-4 war. SAF gold mine strikes introduce deliberate economic warfare targeting RSF revenue; deviation from prior kinetic focus signals funding degradation strategy.

open_findings.headline: Middle East tensions reshape Sudan war economy as Iran-linked supply networks shift
open_findings.why_it_matters: ACLED analysis confirms Middle East regional pressure and shifting supply networks materially reshaping Sudan conflict dynamics, with Iran-adjacent actors adjusting arms and resource flows. Sudan-Iran linkage structurally underweighted in mainstream conflict analysis.

open_findings.headline: Houthis warn of Red Sea intervention if Iran attacked; Iran presses for renewed shipping attacks
open_findings.why_it_matters: Houthi warnings operationally credible given prior anti-ship missile attacks on US Navy vessels. Iran pressing Houthis toward Red Sea attacks if US escalates signals proxy activation as deliberate escalation tool. Threatens 10% global seaborne trade via Bab al-Mandeb.
open_findings.source_name: Council on Foreign Relations Global Conflict Tracker

open_findings.headline: Gulf GDP contraction projections: Qatar and Kuwait up to 14%, Saudi Arabia 3%, UAE 5%
open_findings.why_it_matters: Economic pressure constraining Gulf states capacity to fund proxy positions in Sudan and other theatres. Gulf GDP contraction projections directly constrain external funding for both SAF and RSF, accelerating resource competition.

open_findings.headline: DPRK nuclear acceleration ordered; 7 tests 2026 to April
open_findings.why_it_matters: Notes confirm above-baseline I1/I2/I3 with nuclear and missile activity; warrants Worsening over vague Elevated. DPRK: nearly all satellite analysis is T2; Confirmed confidence rarely achievable.
